source_title: Reference Data Harmonization Webinar (1/4) from Data Foundations

concepts.name: Reference Data
concepts.definition: Data used to categorize other data, often standardized lists of values (e.g., country codes, currency codes).

concepts.name: Data Harmonization
concepts.definition: The process of bringing together data from different sources and providing a consistent view of them, reconciling differences in formats and structures.

concepts.name: Golden Record
concepts.definition: A single, authoritative record for an entity created by merging and de-duplicating data from multiple sources.

steps.details: Catalog all systems and their respective code lists to understand scope.
Map local codes to standard ISO or industry standards where possible.
Create a central dictionary that maps local codes to a master reference set.
Use the harmonized data to create a single source of truth.

code_recipes.name: Python Map Codes to Reference Codes

gotchas.description: Using internal codes as keys instead of standard codes
gotchas.context: Developers often use internal database IDs or legacy codes as keys. When harmonizing, using internal IDs breaks the link to external reference data.
gotchas.solution: Map internal keys to standard IDs (like ISO codes) explicitly in a mapping table.

gotchas.context: One source code might map to multiple standard codes, or multiple source codes map to one standard code.
gotchas.solution: Create explicit one-to-one mapping tables where possible, and define business rules for many-to-one/one-to-many relationships.
